name: ezomero

metadata.name: ezomero
metadata.summary: A suite of convenience functions for working with OMERO. Written and maintained by the Research IT team at The Jackson Laboratory.

documentation:
![](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/TheJacksonLaboratory/ezomero/main/coverage.svg) [![badge-doi](https://img.shields.io/badge/doi-10.1101%2F2023.06.29.546930-purple)](https://doi.org/10.1101/2023.06.29.546930) 

# ezomero
A module with convenience functions for writing Python code that interacts with OMERO.


# Installation

ezomero's dependencies are easily pip-installable from PyPI, except for `zeroc-ice==3.6.5`. For those, we recommend pip-installing using one of the [wheels](https://github.com/glencoesoftware/zeroc-ice-py-linux-x86_64/releases) provided by Glencoe Software (use the one compatible  with your OS/Python version - link provided is for Linux wheels, for more information see [this Glencoe Software blog post](https://www.glencoesoftware.com/blog/2023/12/08/ice-binaries-for-omero.html)).

In general, we **strongly** recommend starting from a clean virtual environment, `pip install`ing `zeroc-ice` from a Glencoe wheel, and only then doing `pip install ezomero`. 

If you want to use `get_table` and `post_table` to/from Pandas dataframes, you need to install `ezomero[tables]` - that install an optional `pandas` dependency. Installing ezomero without this will default `get_table` and `post_table` to use lists of row lists as their default.

# Usage

In general, you will need to create a `BlitzGateway` object using `ezomero.connect()`, then pass the `conn` object to most of these helper functions along with function-specific parameters.


# Documentation

Documentation is available at https://thejacksonlaboratory.github.io/ezomero/

# Development

You will need Docker installed and running to run the tests.

Setup your "omero" python environment with a local ezomero and pytest:
```
> conda activate omero  # Activate your omero environment with conda or pip
(omero) > cd /your_local_clone/ezomero
(omero) > pip install -e .
(omero) > pip install pytest
```

To run the tests, startup the test OMERO server with Docker and run pytest
```
> cd /your_local_clone/ezomero
> docker-compose -f tests/docker-compose.yml up -d
> conda activate omero
(omero) > python -m pytest .\tests
```

functions.name: ezomero._misc.filter_by_filename
documentation:
Filter list of image ids by originalFile name.

Sometimes we know the filename of an image that has been imported into
OMERO but not necessarily the image ID. This is frequently the case when
we want to annotate a recently imported image. This funciton will help
to filter a list of image IDs to only those associated with a particular
filename.

# Parameters

conn

: OMERO connection.

im_ids

: List of OMERO image IDs.

imported_filename

: The full filename (with extension) of the file whose OMERO image
  we are looking for. NOT the path of the image.

across_groups

: Defines cross-group behavior of function - set to
  `False` to disable it.

# Returns

filtered_im_ids

: Filtered list of images with originalFile name matching
  `imported_filename`.

# Notes

This function should be used as a filter on an image list that has been
already narrowed down as much as possible. Note that many different images
in OMERO may share the same filename (e.g., image.tif).

# Examples

```pycon
>>> im_ids = get_image_ids(conn, dataset=303)
>>> im_ids = filter_by_filename(conn, im_ids, "feb_2020.tif")]
```
filters

functions.name: ezomero.json_api.create_json_session
documentation:
Create an OMERO connection using the JSON API

This function will create an OMERO connection by populating certain
parameters for a request using the `requests` library by the
procedure described in the notes below. Note that this function may
ask for user input, so be cautious if using in the context of a script.

# Parameters

user

: OMERO username.

password

: OMERO password.

web_host

: OMERO.web host.

verify

: Whether to verify SSL certificates when making requests.

server_name

: OMERO server name as configured in your OMERO.web installation. For
  a "default" OMERO.web installation, it will normally be 'omero'.

config_path

: Path to directory containing '.ezomero' file that stores connection
  information. If left as `None`, defaults to the home directory as
  determined by Python's `pathlib`.

# Returns

login_rsp

: JSON containing the response to the `POST` request sent for log in.

session

: The effective `requests` session that will be used for further
  requests to the JSON API.

base_url

: Base URL for further requests, retrieved via JSON API request

# Notes

The procedure for choosing parameters for initializing a JSON API session
is as follows:

1. Any parameters given to `create_json_connection` will be used to
   initialize a JSON session

2. If a parameter is not given to this function, populate from variables
   in `os.environ`:

   > - OMERO_USER
   > - OMERO_PASS
   > - OMERO_WEB_HOST

3. If environment variables are not set, try to load from a config file.
   This file should be called '.ezomero'. By default, this function will
   look in the home directory, but `config_path` can be used to specify
   a directory in which to look for '.ezomero'.

   The function `ezomero.store_connection_params` can be used to create
   the '.ezomero' file.

   Note that passwords can not be loaded from the '.ezomero' file. This is
   to discourage storing credentials in a file as cleartext.

4. If any remaining parameters have not been set by the above steps, the
   user is prompted to enter a value for each unset parameter.

Note that, by default, this function will use the latest API version
available, in case multiple versions are offered by the OMERO.web server.

functions.name: ezomero.json_api.get_rendered_jpeg
documentation:
Get a numpy array from a rendered JPEG at given scale factor
of an image.

# Parameters

session

: `requests` session that has been initialized with login parameters.

base_url

: Base URL for further requests, retrieved via JSON API request

img_id

: ID of `Image`.

scale

: Scaling factor for the returned JPEG. `1` returns original size,
  `2` scales each dimension down by half, and so on.

# Returns

pixels

: Array containing pixel values from rendered JPEG of the OMERO image.

# Examples

\# Get a JPEG of an entire image as a numpy array:

```pycon
>>> jpeg_array = get_rendered_jpeg(session, base_url, 314, 1)
```

functions.name: ezomero._posts.post_dataset
documentation:
Create a new dataset.

# Parameters

conn

: OMERO connection.

dataset_name

: Name of the Dataset being created.

project_id

: Id of Project in which to create the Dataset. If no Project is
  specified, the Dataset will be orphaned.

description

: Description for the new Dataset.

across_groups

: Defines cross-group behavior of function - set to
  `False` to disable it.

# Returns

dataset_id

: Id of the dataset that has been created.

# Examples

\# Create a new orphaned Dataset:

```pycon
>>> did = post_dataset(conn, "New Dataset")
>>> did
234
```

\# Create a new Dataset in Project:120:

```pycon
>>> did = post_dataset(conn, "Child of 120", project_id=120)
>>> did
```

functions.name: ezomero._posts.post_image
documentation:
Create a new OMERO image from numpy array.

# Parameters

conn

: OMERO connection.

image

: The numpy array from which a new OMERO image will be created. Note that
  array.ndim must equal 5. The function assumes this `ndarray` uses
  XYZCT ordering.

image_name

: Name of the new image to be created.

description

: Description for the new image.

dataset_id

: Id of the Dataset in which to create the image. If no Dataset is
  specified, an orphaned image will be created.

source_image_id

: If specified, copy this image with metadata, then add pixel data from
  `image` parameter.

channel_list

: Copies metadata from these channels in source image (if specified).

dim_order

: String containing the letters 'x', 'y', 'z', 'c' and 't' in some order,
  specifying the order of dimensions the `image` array was supplied on.

across_groups

: Defines cross-group behavior of function - set to
  `False` to disable it.

# Returns

image_id

: Id of the new image that has been created.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> test_image = np.zeros((200, 200, 20, 3, 1), dtype=np.uint8)
>>> im_id = post_image(conn, test_image, "test", dataset_id=105)
>>> print(im_id)
234
```

functions.name: ezomero._posts.post_map_annotation
documentation:
Create new MapAnnotation and link to an object.

# Parameters

conn

: OMERO connection.

object_type

: OMERO object type, passed to `BlitzGateway.getObjects`

object_id

: ID of object to which the new MapAnnotation will be linked.

kv_dict

: key-value pairs that will be included in the MapAnnotation

ns

: Namespace for the MapAnnotation

across_groups

: Defines cross-group behavior of function - set to
  `False` to disable it.

# Notes

All keys and values are converted to strings before saving in OMERO.
Passing a list of values will result in multiple instances of the key,
one for each value.

# Returns

map_ann_id

: IDs of newly created MapAnnotation

# Examples

```pycon
>>> ns = 'jax.org/jax/example/namespace'
>>> d = {'species': 'human',
...      'occupation': ['time traveler', 'soldier'].
...      'first name': 'Kyle',
...      'surname': 'Reese'}
>>> post_map_annotation(conn, "Image", 56, d, ns)
234
```

functions.name: ezomero._posts.post_file_annotation
documentation:
Create new FileAnnotation and link to images.

# Parameters

conn

: OMERO connection.

file_path

: local path to file to be added as FileAnnotation

ns

: Namespace for the FileAnnotation

object_type

: OMERO object type, passed to `BlitzGateway.getObject`

object_id

: ID of object to which the new FileAnnotation will be linked.

mimetype

: String of the form 'type/subtype', usable for a MIME content-type
  header.

description

: File description to be added to FileAnnotation

across_groups

: Defines cross-group behavior of function - set to
  `False` to disable it.

# Notes

All keys and values are converted to strings before saving in OMERO.

# Returns

file_ann_id

: IDs of newly created FileAnnotation

# Examples

```pycon
>>> ns = 'jax.org/jax/example/namespace'
>>> path = '/home/user/Downloads/file_ann.txt'
>>> post_file_annotation(conn, path, ns, "Image", 56)
234
```

functions.name: ezomero._posts.post_project
documentation:
Create a new project.

# Parameters

conn

: OMERO connection.

project_name

: Name of the new object to be created.

description

: Description for the new Project.

# Returns

project_id

: Id of the new Project.

# Notes

Project will be created in the Group specified in the connection. Group can
be changed using `conn.SERVICE_OPTS.setOmeroGroup`.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> project_id = post_project(conn, "My New Project")
>>> print(project_id)
238
```

functions.name: ezomero._posts.post_screen

Create a new screen.

# Parameters

conn

: OMERO connection.

screen_name

: Name of the new object to be created.

description

: Description for the new Screen.

# Returns

screen_id

: Id of the new Screen.

# Notes

Screen will be created in the Group specified in the connection. Group can
be changed using `conn.SERVICE_OPTS.setOmeroGroup`.

# Examples

```pycon

>>> print(screen_id)
238
```

functions.name: ezomero._posts.post_roi
documentation:
Create new ROI from a list of shapes and link to an image.

# Parameters

conn

: OMERO connection.

image_id

: IDs of the image to which the new ROI will be linked.

shapes

: List of shape objects associated with the new ROI.

name

: Name for the new ROI.

description

: Description of the new ROI.

# Returns

ROI_id

: ID of newly created ROI

# Examples

```pycon
>>> shapes = list()
>>> point = Point(x=30.6, y=80.4)
>>> shapes.append(point)
>>> rectangle = Rectangle(x=50.0,
                          y=51.3,
                          width=90,
                          height=40,
                          z=3,
                          label='The place',
                          fill_color=(255, 10, 10, 150),
                          stroke_color=(255, 0, 0, 0),
                          stroke_width=2)
>>> shapes.append(rectangle)
>>> post_roi(conn, 23, shapes, name='My Cell',
             description='Very important')
234
```

functions.name: ezomero._posts.post_table
documentation:
Create new table and link it to an OMERO object.

# Parameters

conn

: OMERO connection.

object_type

: OMERO object type, passed to `BlitzGateway.getObjects`

object_id

: ID of object to which the new Table will be linked.

table

: Object containing the actual table. It can be either a list of
  row-lists or a pandas Dataframe in case the optional pandas dependency
  was installed. Note that each column should be of a single type;
  mixed-type columns will be ignored. Types supported: int, string,
  float, boolean.

title

: Title for the table. If none is specified, a `Table:ID` name is picked,
  with a random UUID. Note that table names need to be unique!

headers

: Whether the first line of the `table` object should be interpreted
  as column headers or not. Defaults to `True` and is ignored for pandas
  Dataframes.

# Returns

TableFile_id

: ID of newly created FileAnnotation containing the new Table.

# Notes

Currently not working with `across_groups` - the `OriginalFile` seems to
ignore setting groups dynamically and always does it on the original
connection group, causing issues.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> columns = ['ID', 'X', 'Y']
>>> table = [columns, [1, 10, 20], [2, 30, 40]]
>>> post_table(conn, table, "Image", 99, title='My Table', headers=True)
234
```

functions.name: ezomero._posts.create_columns

functions.name: ezomero._posts._shape_to_omero_shape

functions.name: ezomero._importer.ezimport
documentation:
Entry point that creates Importer and runs import.

# Parameters

conn

: OMERO connection.

target

: Path to the import target to be imported into OMERO.

project

: The name or ID of the Project data will be imported into.

dataset

: The name or ID of the Dataset data will be imported into.

screen

: The name or ID of the Screen data will be imported into.

ann

: Dictionary with key-value pairs to be added to imported images.

ns

: Namespace for the added key-value pairs.

args, kwargs

: You can also add any extra arguments you would like to pass to
  `omero import` to the end of the argument list in `ezimport`.
  For example, an in-place import can be done by adding
  `transfer="ln_s"` as an extra argument when calling `` ezimport` ``.

# Returns

plate_ids or image_ids

: The ids of the Images/Plates that were generated by importing the
  specified target.

# Notes

This function is EXPERIMENTAL and has seen minimal testing. Use at
your own risk! We do not recommend using this in production.
import

functions.name: ezomero._importer.set_or_create_project
documentation:
Create or set a Project of interest.

If argument is a string, creates a new Project with that name. If it is
an integer, sets that Project ID as the Project of interest.
Parameter
\---------
conn : `omero.gateway.BlitzGateway` object.

> OMERO connection.

project

: The name or ID of the Project needed.

# Returns

project_id

: The id of the Project that was either found or created.

functions.name: ezomero._importer.set_or_create_dataset
documentation:
Create or set a Dataset of interest.

If argument is a string, creates a new Dataset with that name. If it is
an integer, sets that Dataset ID as the Dataset of interest. If
`project_id` is specified, new Dataset will be created in that Project.
Parameter
\---------
conn : `omero.gateway.BlitzGateway` object.

> OMERO connection.

project_id

: Id of Project in which to find/create Dataset.

dataset

: The name or ID of the Dataset needed.

# Returns

dataset_id

: The id of the Dataset that was either found or created.

functions.name: ezomero._importer.set_or_create_screen
documentation:
Create or set a Screen of interest.

If argument is a string, creates a new Screen with that name. If it is
an integer, sets that Screen ID as the Screen of interest.
Parameter
\---------
conn : `omero.gateway.BlitzGateway` object.

> OMERO connection.

screen

: The name or ID of the Screen needed.

# Returns

screen_id

: The id of the Screen that was either found or created.

functions.name: ezomero._importer.multi_post_map_annotation
documentation:
Create a single new MapAnnotation and link to multiple images.
Parameters
\----------
conn : `omero.gateway.BlitzGateway` object

> OMERO connection.

object_type

: OMERO object type, passed to `BlitzGateway.getObjects`

object_ids

: IDs of objects to which the new MapAnnotation will be linked.

kv_dict

: key-value pairs that will be included in the MapAnnotation

ns

: Namespace for the MapAnnotation

# Notes

All keys and values are converted to strings before saving in OMERO.
Returns
\-------
map_ann_id : int

> IDs of newly created MapAnnotation

# Examples

```pycon
>>> ns = 'jax.org/jax/example/namespace'
>>> d = {'species': 'human',
         'occupation': ['time traveler', 'soldier'],
         'first name': 'Kyle',
         'surname': 'Reese'}
>>> multi_post_map_annotation(conn, "Image", [23,56,78], d, ns)
234
```

classes.name: ezomero._importer.Importer

methods.name: ezomero._importer.Importer.get_my_image_ids
documentation:
Get the Ids of imported images.

Note that this will not find images if they have not been imported.
Also, while image_ids are returned, this method also sets
`self.image_ids`.
Returns
\-------
image_ids : list of ints

> Ids of images imported from the specified client path, which
> itself is derived from `self.file_path` and `self.filename`.

methods.name: ezomero._importer.Importer.get_plate_ids
documentation:
Get the Ids of imported plates.
Note that this will not find plates if they have not been imported.
Also, while plate_ids are returned, this method also sets
`self.plate_ids`.
Returns
\-------
plate_ids : list of ints

> Ids of plates imported from the specified client path, which
> itself is derived from `self.file_path` and `self.filename`.

Returns

methods.name: ezomero._importer.Importer.annotate_plates

methods.name: ezomero._importer.Importer.ezimport
documentation:
Import file.
Returns
-------
import_status : boolean
    True if OMERO import returns a 0 exit status, else False.

Class for managing OMERO imports using OMERO CLI.

# Parameters

conn

: OMERO connection.

file_path

: Path to the import target to be imported into OMERO.

project

: The name or ID of the Project data will be imported into.

dataset

: The name or ID of the Dataset data will be imported into.

screen

: The name or ID of the Screen data will be imported into.

ann

: Dictionary with key-value pairs to be added to imported images.

ns

: Namespace for the added key-value pairs.

host

: Hostname of the OMERO server to which data will be imported.

port

: Port of the OMERO server to which data will be imported.

args, kwargs

: Receives the `*args`, `**kwargs` from `ezimport` to pass it
  onto `omero import`.

Important notes:
1\) Setting `project` also requires setting `dataset`. Failing to do so
will raise a ValueError.
2\) To annotate images, both `ann` and `ns` need to be set. If one of
them is not set, no annotations will be made.
3\) For automating purposes, the arguments `host` and `port` can be set,
avoiding a user prompt for that info. Both need to be set to bypass that
prompt.
4\) The returned image IDs correspond to ALL image IDs accessible to this
user that have the same `ClientPath`, i.e., that have the same file
name and have been imported from the same folder. In production, this
should be a rare occurrence, but please keep that in mind if you are
getting more image IDs than you were expecting!

functions.name: ezomero._ezomero.get_default_args
documentation:
Retrieves the default arguments of a function.

Parameters
----------
func : function
    Function whose signature we want to inspect

Returns
-------
_ : dict
    Key-value pairs of argument name and value.

functions.name: ezomero._ezomero.do_across_groups
documentation:
Decorator functional for making functions work across
OMERO groups.

Parameters
----------
f : function
    Function that will be decorated

Returns
-------
wrapper : Object
    Return value of the decorated function.

functions.name: ezomero._ezomero.put_map_annotation
documentation:
Update an existing map annotation with new values (kv pairs)

# Parameters

conn

: OMERO connection.

map_ann_id

: ID of map annotation whose values (kv pairs) will be replaced.

kv_dict

: New values (kv pairs) for the MapAnnotation.

ns

: New namespace for the MapAnnotation. If left as None, the old
  namespace will be used.

across_groups

: Defines cross-group behavior of function - set to
  `False` to disable it.

# Notes

All keys and values are converted to strings before saving in OMERO.

# Returns

Returns None.

# Examples

\# Change only the values of an existing map annotation:

```pycon
>>> new_values = {'key1': 'value1', 'key2': ['value2', 'value3']}
>>> put_map_annotation(conn, 15, new_values)
```

\# Change both the values and namespace of an existing map annotation:

```pycon
>>> put_map_annotation(conn, 16, new_values, 'test_v2')
```
puts

functions.name: ezomero._ezomero.put_description
documentation:
Update an existing object description with a new value (string)

# Parameters

conn

: OMERO connection.

obj_type: str

: The OMERO object type to have its description changed. Valid object
  types are 'Image', 'Dataset', 'Project', 'FileAnnotation',
  'CommentAnnotation', 'MapAnnotation', 'TagAnnotation', 'Plate',
  'Screen' and 'Roi'.

obj_id

: ID of the object whose description will be replaced.

desc

: New description for the object.

across_groups

: Defines cross-group behavior of function - set to
  `False` to disable it.

# Returns

Returns None.

# Examples

\# Change an image description:

```pycon
>>> new_desc = "This is a new description"
>>> put_description(conn, 'Image', 15, new_desc)
```

\# Change a TagAnnotation description:

```pycon
>>> put_description(conn, 'TagAnnotation', 16, 'new tag description')
```

functions.name: ezomero._ezomero.connect
documentation:
Create an OMERO connection

This function will create an OMERO connection by populating certain
parameters for `omero.gateway.BlitzGateway` initialization by the
procedure described in the notes below. Note that this function may
ask for user input, so be cautious if using in the context of a script.

Finally, don't forget to close the connection `conn.close()` when it
is no longer needed!

# Parameters

user

: OMERO username.

password

: OMERO password.

group

: OMERO group.

host

: OMERO.server host.

port

: OMERO port.

secure

: Whether to create a secure session.

config_path

: Path to directory containing '.ezomero' file that stores connection
  information. If left as `None`, defaults to the home directory as
  determined by Python's `pathlib`.

# Returns

conn

: OMERO connection, if successful. Otherwise an error is logged and
  returns None.

# Notes

The procedure for choosing parameters for `omero.gateway.BlitzGateway`
initialization is as follows:

1. Any parameters given to `ezomero.connect` will be used to initialize
   `omero.gateway.BlitzGateway`

2. If a parameter is not given to `ezomero.connect`, populate from
   variables in `os.environ`:

   > - OMERO_USER
   > - OMERO_PASS
   > - OMERO_GROUP
   > - OMERO_HOST
   > - OMERO_PORT
   > - OMERO_SECURE

3. If environment variables are not set, try to load from a config file.
   This file should be called '.ezomero'. By default, this function will
   look in the home directory, but `config_path` can be used to specify
   a directory in which to look for '.ezomero'.

   The function `ezomero.store_connection_params` can be used to create
   the '.ezomero' file.

   Note that passwords can not be loaded from the '.ezomero' file. This is
   to discourage storing credentials in a file as cleartext.

4. If any remaining parameters have not been set by the above steps, the
   user is prompted to enter a value for each unset parameter.
functions for managing connection context and service options.

functions.name: ezomero._ezomero.store_connection_params
documentation:
Save OMERO connection parameters in a file.

This function creates a config file ('.ezomero') in which
certain OMERO parameters are stored, to make it easier to create
`omero.gateway.BlitzGateway` objects.

# Parameters

user

: OMERO username.

group

: OMERO group.

host

: OMERO.server host.

port

: OMERO port.

secure

: Whether to create a secure session.

web_host

: Whether to save a web host address got JSON connections as well. If
  `False`, will skip it; it `True`, will prompt user for it; if it is
  a `str`, will save that value to `OMERO_WEB_HOST`.

config_path

: Path to directory that will contain the '.ezomero' file. If left as
  `None`, defaults to the home directory as determined by Python's
  `pathlib`.

functions.name: ezomero._ezomero.set_group
documentation:
Safely switch OMERO group.

This function will change the user's current group to that specified by
`group_id`, but only if the user is a member of that group. This is a
"safer" way to do this than `conn.SERVICE_OPTS.setOmeroGroup`, which will
allow switching to a group that a user does not have permissions, which can
lead to server-side errors.

# Parameters

conn

: OMERO connection.

group_id

: The id of the group to switch to.

# Returns

change_status

: Returns `True` if group is changed, otherwise returns `False`.

classes.name: ezomero.rois.ezShape
documentation:
Generic dataclass used to create an OMERO Shape.

A dataclass used to represent any shape and create an OMERO equivalent.
This class has no fields; it's just extended by all ROIs.
This dataclass is frozen and should not be modified after instantiation

classes.name: ezomero.rois.Point
documentation:
A dataclass used to create an OMERO Point.

A dataclass used to represent a Point shape and create an OMERO equivalent.
This dataclass is frozen and should not be modified after instantiation

# Parameters

x

: The x axis position of the point shape in pixels.

y

: The y axis position of the point shape in pixels.

z

: The z position of the point in pixels. Note this is the z plane to
  which the shape is linked and not the sub-voxel resolution position of
  your shape. If `None`, the Point will not be linked to any z plane.
  Default is `None`.

c

: The channel index to which the shape is linked.
  If None, the Point will not be linked to any channel. Default is
  `None`.

t

: The time frame to which the shape is linked.
  If `None`, the Point will not be linked to any time frame.
  Default is `None`.

label

: The label of the shape. Default is `None`.

fill_color

: The color that will be used as the fill/background of this Shape.
  Format is RGBA, 0-255.
  If `None`, we default to (0, 0, 0, 0) (i.e. transparent) in
  `ezomero.get_shape()`.
  Default is `None`.

stroke_color

: The color that will be used as the stroke/perimeter of this Shape.
  Format is RGBA, 0-255.
  If `None`, we default to (255, 255, 0, 255) (i.e. yellow, following
  OMERO.iViewer default) in `ezomero.get_shape()`.
  Default is `None`.

stroke_width

: The line width used for the stroke/perimeter of this Shape.
  If `None`, we default to 1.0 in `ezomero.get_shape()`.
  Default is `None`.

classes.name: ezomero.rois.Line
documentation:
A dataclass used to create an OMERO Line.

A dataclass used to represent a Line shape and create an OMERO equivalent.

# Parameters

markerEnd

fill_color

  If `None`, we default to (0, 0, 0, 0) (i.e. transparent) in
  `ezomero.get_shape()`.
  Default is `None`.

stroke_color

stroke_width

classes.name: ezomero.rois.Polygon
documentation:

  Default is `None`.

stroke_color

functions.name: ezomero._gets.get_image
documentation:
Get omero image object along with pixels as a numpy array.

# Parameters

conn

: OMERO connection.

image_id

: Id of the image to get.

no_pixels

: If true, no pixel data is returned, only the OMERO image object.
  Default is `False`.

start_coords

: Starting coordinates for each axis for the pixel region to be returned
  if `no_pixels` is `False` (assumes XYZCT ordering). If `None`, the zero
  coordinate is used for each axis. Default is None.

axis_lengths

: Lengths for each axis for the pixel region to be returned if
  `no_pixels` is `False`. If `None`, the lengths will be set such that
  the entire possible range of pixels is returned. Default is None.

xyzct

: Option to return array with dimensional ordering XYZCT. If `False`, the
  `skimage` preferred ordering will be used (TZYXC). Default is False.

pad

: If `axis_lengths` values would result in out-of-bounds indices, pad
  pixel array with zeros. Otherwise, such an operation will raise an
  exception. Ignored if `no_pixels` is True.

pyramid_level

: If image has multiple pyramid levels and this argument is set, pixels
  are returned at the chosen resolution level, and all other arguments
  apply to that level as well. We follow the usual convention of `0` as
  full-resolution.

dim_order

: String containing the letters 'x', 'y', 'z', 'c' and 't' in some order,
  specifying the order of dimensions to be returned by the function.
  If specified, ignores the value of the 'xyzct' variable.

across_groups

: Defines cross-group behavior of function - set to
  `False` to disable it.

# Returns

image

: OMERO image object.

pixels

: Array containing pixel values from OMERO image. Can be a subregion
  of the image if `start_coords` and `axis_lengths` are specified.

# Notes

Regardless of whether `xyzct` is `True` or `dim_order` is set, the numpy
array is created as TZYXC, for performance reasons. If `xyzct` is `True`
or `dim_order` is set, the returned `pixels` array is actually a view
of the original TZYXC array.

# Examples

\# Get an entire image as a numpy array:

```pycon
>>> im_object, im_array = get_image(conn, 314)
```

\# Get a subregion of an image as a numpy array:

```pycon
>>> im_o, im_a = get_image(conn, 314, start_coords=(40, 50, 4, 0, 0),
...                        axis_lengths=(256, 256, 12, 10, 10))
```

\# Get only the OMERO image object, no pixels:

```pycon
>>> im_object, _ = get_image(conn, 314, no_pixels=True)
>>> im_object.getId()
314
```
gets

functions.name: ezomero._gets.get_image_ids
documentation:
Return a list of image ids based on image container

If no container is specified, function will return orphans.

# Parameters

conn

: OMERO connection.

project

: ID of Project from which to return image IDs. This will return IDs of
  all images contained in all child Datasets of the specified Project.

dataset

: ID of Dataset from which to return image IDs.

plate

: ID of Plate from which to return image IDs. This will return IDs of
  all images contained in all Wells belonging to the specified Plate.

well

: ID of Well from which to return image IDs.

plate_acquisition

: ID of Plate acquisition from which to return image IDs.

annotation

: ID of Annotation from which to return image IDs. This will return IDs
  of all images linked to the specified annotation.

across_groups

: Defines cross-group behavior of function - set to
  `False` to disable it.

# Returns

im_ids

: List of image IDs contained in the specified container.

# Notes

User and group information comes from the `conn` object. Be sure to use
`ezomero.set_group` to specify group prior to passing
the `conn` object to this function.

Only one of Project, Dataset, Plate, Well, Plate acquisition or Annotation
can be specified. If none of those are specified, orphaned images are
returned.

# Examples

\# Return orphaned images:

```pycon
>>> orphans = get_image_ids(conn)
```

\# Return IDs of all images from Dataset with ID 448:

```pycon
>>> ds_ims = get_image_ids(conn, dataset=448)
```

\# Return IDs of all images annotated with Tag ID 876:

```pycon
>>> tag_ims = get_image_ids(conn, annotation=876)
```

functions.name: ezomero._gets.get_project_ids
documentation:
Return a list with IDs for all available Projects.

# Parameters

conn

: OMERO connection.

annotation

: ID of Annotation from which to return project IDs. This will return IDs
  of all projects linked to the specified annotation.

across_groups

: Defines cross-group behavior of function - set to
  `False` to disable it.

# Returns

proj_ids

: List of project IDs accessible by current user.

# Examples

\# Return IDs of all projects accessible by current user:

```pycon
>>> proj_ids = get_project_ids(conn)
```

\# Return IDs of all projects annotated with tag id 576:

```pycon
>>> proj_ids = get_project_ids(conn, annotation=576)
```

functions.name: ezomero._gets.get_dataset_ids
documentation:
Return a list of dataset ids based on project ID.

If no project or annotation is specified, function will return
orphan datasets.

# Parameters

conn

: OMERO connection.

project

: ID of Project from which to return dataset IDs. This will return IDs of
  all datasets contained in the specified Project.

annotation

: ID of Annotation from which to return dataset IDs. This will return IDs
  of all datasets linked to the specified annotation.

across_groups

: Defines cross-group behavior of function - set to
  `False` to disable it.

# Returns

ds_ids

: List of dataset IDs contained in the specified project.

# Examples

\# Return orphaned datasets:

```pycon
>>> orphans = get_dataset_ids(conn)
```

\# Return IDs of all datasets from Project with ID 224:

```pycon
>>> ds_ids = get_dataset_ids(conn, project=224)
```

functions.name: ezomero._gets.get_screen_ids

# Examples

```pycon

```

\# Return IDs of all screens annotated with tag id 913:

```pycon
>>> proj_ids = get_screen_ids(conn, annotation=913)
```

functions.name: ezomero._gets.get_plate_ids

```pycon

```

\# Return IDs of all plates from Screen with ID 224:

```pycon

```

functions.name: ezomero._gets.get_map_annotation_ids
documentation:
Get IDs of map annotations associated with an object

# Parameters

conn

: OMERO connection.

object_type

: OMERO object type, passed to `BlitzGateway.getObject`

object_id

: ID of object of `object_type`.

ns

: Namespace with which to filter results

across_groups

: Defines cross-group behavior of function - set to
  `False` to disable it.

# Returns

map_ann_ids : list of ints

# Examples

\# Return IDs of all map annotations belonging to an image:

```pycon
>>> map_ann_ids = get_map_annotation_ids(conn, 'Image', 42)
```

\# Return IDs of map annotations with namespace "test" linked to a Dataset:

```pycon
>>> map_ann_ids = get_map_annotation_ids(conn, 'Dataset', 16, ns='test')
```

functions.name: ezomero._gets.get_tag_ids
documentation:
Get IDs of tag annotations associated with an object

# Parameters

conn

: OMERO connection.

object_type

: OMERO object type, passed to `BlitzGateway.getObject`

object_id

: ID of object of `object_type`.

ns

: Namespace with which to filter results

across_groups

: Defines cross-group behavior of function - set to
  `False` to disable it.

# Returns

tag_ids : list of ints

# Examples

\# Return IDs of all tags linked to an image:

```pycon
>>> tag_ids = get_tag_ids(conn, 'Image', 42)
```

\# Return IDs of tags with namespace "test" linked to a Dataset:

```pycon
>>> tag_ids = get_tag_ids(conn, 'Dataset', 16, ns='test')
```

functions.name: ezomero._gets.get_comment_annotation_ids
documentation:
Get IDs of comment annotations associated with an object

# Parameters

conn

: OMERO connection.

object_type

: OMERO object type, passed to `BlitzGateway.getObject`

object_id

: ID of object of `object_type`.

ns

: Namespace with which to filter results

across_groups

: Defines cross-group behavior of function - set to
  `False` to disable it.

# Returns

comment_ids : list of ints

# Examples

\# Return IDs of all comments linked to an image:

```pycon
>>> comment_ids = get_comment_ids(conn, 'Image', 42)
```

\# Return IDs of comments with namespace "test" linked to a Dataset:

```pycon
>>> tag_ids = get_tag_ids(conn, 'Dataset', 16, ns='test')
```

conn

: OMERO connection.

object_type

: OMERO object type, passed to `BlitzGateway.getObject`

object_id

ns

across_groups

: Defines cross-group behavior of function - set to
  `False` to disable it.

# Returns

file_ann_ids : list of ints

# Examples

\# Return IDs of all file annotations linked to an image:

```pycon

```

\# Return IDs of file annotations with namespace "test" linked to a Dataset:

```pycon
>>> file_ann_ids = get_file_annotation_ids(conn, 'Dataset', 16, ns='test')
```

functions.name: ezomero._gets.get_well_id
documentation:
Get ID of well based on plate ID, row, and column

: OMERO connection.

: ID of plate for which the well ID is needed

row

: Row of well (zero-based indexing)

column

# Returns

well_id

: ID of well being queried.

Get IDs of ROIs associated with an Image

Get IDs of shapes associated with an ROI

roi_id

functions.name: ezomero._gets.get_map_annotation
documentation:
Get the value of a map annotation object

# Parameters

conn

: OMERO connection.

map_ann_id

: ID of map annotation to get.

across_groups

: Defines cross-group behavior of function - set to
  `False` to disable it.

# Returns

kv_dict

: The value of the specified map annotation object, as a Python dict.
  If kv-pairs with the same key exist, the corresponding dict value
  will be a list.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> ma_dict = get_map_annotation(conn, 62)
>>> print(ma_dict)
{'testkey': 'testvalue', 'testkey2': ['testvalue2'. 'testvalue3']}
```

functions.name: ezomero._gets.get_tag
documentation:
Get the value of a tag annotation object

# Parameters

conn

: OMERO connection.

tag_id

: ID of tag annotation to get.

across_groups

: Defines cross-group behavior of function - set to
  `False` to disable it.

# Returns

tag

: The value of the specified tag annotation object.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> tag = get_tag(conn, 62)
>>> print(tag)
This_is_a_tag
```

functions.name: ezomero._gets.get_comment_annotation
documentation:
Get the value of a comment annotation object

# Parameters

conn

: OMERO connection.

comment_id

: ID of comment annotation to get.

across_groups

: Defines cross-group behavior of function - set to
  `False` to disable it.

# Returns

comment

: The value of the specified tag annotation object.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> comment = get_tag(conn, 62)
>>> print(comment)
This is a comment
```

functions.name: ezomero._gets.get_file_annotation
documentation:
Get the value of a file annotation object

# Parameters

conn

: OMERO connection.

file_ann_id

: ID of file annotation to get.

folder_path

: Path where file annotation will be saved. Defaults to local script
  directory.

across_groups

: Defines cross-group behavior of function - set to
  `False` to disable it.

# Returns

file_path

: The path to the created file.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> attch_path = get_file_annotation(conn,
...                                  62,
...                                  folder_path='/home/user/Downloads')
>>> print(attch_path)
'/home/user/Downloads/attachment.txt'
```

functions.name: ezomero._gets.get_group_id
documentation:
Get ID of a group based on group name.

Must be an exact match. Case sensitive.

# Parameters

conn

: OMERO connection.

group_name

: Name of the group for which an ID is to be returned.

# Returns

group_id

: ID of the OMERO group. Returns `None` if group cannot be found.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> get_group_id(conn, "Research IT")
304
```

functions.name: ezomero._gets.get_user_id
documentation:
Get ID of a user based on user name.

Must be an exact match. Case sensitive.

# Parameters

conn

: OMERO connection.

user_name

: Name of the user for which an ID is to be returned.

# Returns

user_id

: ID of the OMERO user. Returns `None` if group cannot be found.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> get_user_id(conn, "jaxl")
35
```

functions.name: ezomero._gets.get_original_filepaths
documentation:
Get paths to original files for specified image.

# Parameters

conn

: OMERO connection.

image_id

: ID of image for which filepath info is to be returned.

fpath

: Specify whether you want to return path to file in the managed
  repository ('repo') or the path from which the image was imported
  ('client'). The latter is useful for images that were imported by
  the "in place" method. Defaults to 'repo'.

across_groups

: Defines cross-group behavior of function - set to
  `False` to disable it.

# Notes

The ManagedRepository ('repo') paths are relative, whereas the client paths
are absolute.

The client path may not be accessible if the image was not imported using
"in place" imports (e.g., `transfer=ln_s`).

# Returns

original_filepaths : list of str

# Examples

\# Return (relative) path of file in ManagedRepository:

```pycon
>>> get_original_filepaths(conn, 745)
['djme_2/2020-06/16/13-38-36.468/PJN17_083_07.ndpi']
```

\# Return client path (location of file when it was imported):

```pycon
>>> get_original_filepaths(conn, 2201, fpath='client')
['/client/omero/smith_lab/stack2/PJN17_083_07.ndpi']
```

functions.name: ezomero._gets.get_pyramid_levels
documentation:
Get number of pyramid levels associated with an Image

# Parameters

conn

: OMERO connection.

image_id

: ID of `Image`.

across_groups

: Defines cross-group behavior of function - set to
  `False` to disable it.

# Returns

levels

: Pyramidal levels available for this image, with number of
  pixels for X and Y axes.

# Examples

\# Return pyramid levels associated to an image:

```pycon
>>> lvls = get_pyramid_levels(conn, 42)
[(2048, 1600), (1024, 800), (512, 400), (256, 200)]
```

functions.name: ezomero._gets.get_table
documentation:
Get a table from its FileAnnotation object.

# Parameters

conn

: OMERO connection.

file_ann_id

: ID of FileAnnotation table to get.

across_groups

: Defines cross-group behavior of function - set to
  `False` to disable it.

# Returns

table

: Object containing the actual table. It can be either a list of
  row-lists or a pandas Dataframe in case the optional pandas dependency
  was installed.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> table = get_table(conn, 62)
>>> print(table[0])
['ID', 'X', 'Y']
```

functions.name: ezomero._gets.get_shape
documentation:
Get an ezomero shape object from an OMERO Shape id

# Parameters

conn

: OMERO connection.

shape_id

: ID of shape to get.

across_groups

: Defines cross-group behavior of function - set to
  `False` to disable it.

# Returns

shape

: An object of one of ezomero shape classes

# Notes

`fill_color` for the Shape defaults to (0, 0, 0, 0) in case the original
Shape doesn't have one.
`stroke_color` for the Shape defaults to (255, 255, 0, 255) in case the
original Shape doesn't have one.
`stroke_width` for the Shape defaults to 1 in case the original Shape
doesn't have one.
Examples
\--------
\>>> shape = get_shape(conn, 634443)
